updates.source: Anthropic Official (docs.anthropic.com / Claude Apps release notes)
updates.title: New in Claude Cowork: Teach Claude a Skill by Recording Your Screen
updates.summary: Anthropic launched 'Record a Skill' inside Claude Cowork, available to Pro, Max, and Team plan subscribers via the Claude desktop app. Users screen-record a workflow while narrating their actions and Claude automatically converts the demonstration into a structured, reusable automation skill saved to their skill library — no prompt engineering required. The feature targets recurring tasks like report generation, spreadsheet workflows, and file organization, eliminating repetitive manual prompting.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via official @claudeai X post (July 21). Corroborated by Android Authority, Android Headlines, The Decoder, Dataconomy (July 22), Digit.in, and multiple other independent outlets. Feature mechanics (screen capture + voice narration → structured skill) are consistently described across all sources. No red flags or contradictions.

instagram.hook: You can now TRAIN Claude like a new employee — just by recording your screen once 🎬

updates.title: I let Claude clean up my Windows install, and it found bloat I didn't even know existed
updates.summary: An XDA Developers writer used Claude (via Claude Code on Windows) to audit and clean a bloated Windows installation. Claude identified and safely removed 5.98 GB from the Windows component store, 28 GB from an abandoned WSL sandbox, and additional storage from other redundant files — sources of bloat the writer had not previously noticed. The article positions Claude as a surprisingly capable and safe Windows maintenance tool.
updates.accuracy_notes: Reported as a first-person user experience piece from XDA. Storage figures (5.98 GB, 28 GB WSL) are specific and plausible. Cannot independently verify exact file sizes, but the claim that Claude Code can identify Windows storage waste is consistent with its known agentic computer-use capabilities. Accuracy risk is low — framed as personal experience, not universal benchmark.

updates.title: Claude for Teachers: Data Privacy Policy Clarification Added
updates.summary: Anthropic updated the Claude for Teachers product page and documentation to add an explicit note clarifying that whether and how educational data may be used in Claude for Teachers is governed by individual district and state policies — not unilaterally by Anthropic. This update follows the July 14 launch of Claude for Teachers, which provides free premium Claude access to verified US K-12 educators, amid criticism from education watchdogs about data privacy concerns.
updates.accuracy_notes: Data policy update confirmed via search results citing July 21, 2026 as the update date. Context (critics raising concerns) corroborated by EdWeek and The 74 Million coverage of the original launch. The policy note clarification is reactive — Anthropic is softening concerns rather than changing actual data practices. Accuracy risk: low for the factual update; higher if framed as a full data policy overhaul (it is not).

instagram.hook: Free AI for teachers — but what happens to student data? Anthropic just addressed this directly.

updates.title: New API Beta Header: agent-memory-2026-07-22 Changes Memory Listing Behavior
updates.summary: Anthropic published a new API beta header, `agent-memory-2026-07-22`, for the Managed Agents platform. When this header is sent, the GET /v1/memory_stores/{memory_store_id}/memories endpoint returns memories in a stable, server-defined order, and the `order_by` and `order` request parameters are ignored. This aligns the endpoint's behavior with the managed-agents-2026-04-01 header's existing behavior for the same endpoint.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via web search referencing docs.anthropic.com API release notes dated July 22, 2026. Technical change is internally consistent with how Anthropic typically versions beta API behaviors. Low accuracy risk — this is a factual API changelog entry. Content potential is limited to developer-focused audiences.
